title: unreleased (loose container)
label: self-released
notes: Container folder at `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/unreleased/` holding two mixed-period MP3s that don't fit any dated session folder: `bip bop.mp3` (5.09 MB, mtime 2010-10-30) and `lust howls.mp3` (8.10 MB, mtime 2013-02-19, with an Ableton .asd sidecar from 2015-09-15). The folder's own mtime is 2015-09-15, consistent with Colin having swept loose MP3s into it during a late cleanup rather than recording them together. Distinct from the separate `unreleased fall 08/` folder (see [[releases/alphabets-unreleased-fall-08]]), which is period-specific.
body:
Loose "unreleased" container — not a coherent release, but a late-career
folder-of-loose-ends that Colin himself appears to have curated. The two
tracks span October 2010 (peak BIEBER JERBOA / CAVE RAVE-adjacent era) and
February 2013 (near the tail of the Alphabets cadence), so this is not a
session remnant — it's a bin of keep-worthy orphans from across the
Alphabets run.

## Confirmed tracks (from archive filenames)

- bip bop.mp3 _(mtime 2010-10-30, 5.09 MB)_
- lust howls.mp3 _(mtime 2013-02-19, 8.10 MB; .asd sidecar 2015-09-15)_

## Notes

The .asd sidecar on `lust howls.mp3` means Colin had that track loaded in
Ableton as late as September 2015 — possibly reworking it, possibly just
auditioning it.

**`lust howls` is a recurring motif, not a one-off** _(found 2026-04-24)._
A filename sweep of `_Documentation/Music/alphabets/` for the phrase
turned up multiple sibling tracks across the alphabets era:

- `07_08 [_]/lust howls in the afternoon.mp3` (2007–2008)
- `07_08 [_]/lust howls and calls for mating like locusts do.m4a` (2007–2008)
- `09 [_]/lust calls and sex dance.m4a` (related "lust calls" variant, 2009)
- `unreleased/lust howls.mp3` (this entry, 2013)

The 2013 track is the latest in a 5-year arc. A broader "howl" theme also
runs through the catalog — "robo goats howl and bang" (fantazia2010),
"plesiosaur howl outs and swim bump" (BESTUV09 + sep09), "robodogs nighttime
cage howls" (DEC09SCIFI). For website purposes, this is a candidate
"motif/series" page rather than treating any one track in isolation.

The container naming (plain `unreleased`) distinguishes this from all the
period-specific "unreleased X" folders — `unreleased fall 08`,
`unreleased_dec08` patterns, etc. Those are per-session; this is a curated
overflow.
